---
title: "Axen_et_al"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-26"
---

```{r}
gp_df <- df_val %>%
  transmute(
    tf = tf,
    pred_gam = pred,
    resid = resid_response,
    lon = lon_plot,
    lat = lat,
    time = time,
    time_ka = -time / 1000
  ) 
```

```{r}
set.seed(1)

n_train <- 2500

gp_train <- gp_df %>%
  sample_n(min(n_train, nrow(gp_df)))

cat("GP-Trainingspunkte:", nrow(gp_train), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Anisotroper Matérn-3/2 Raumkernel
# rho_lon und rho_lat sind Längenskalen in Grad.
matern32_space <- function(lon1, lat1, lon2, lat2, rho_lon, rho_lat) {
  
  dx <- outer(lon1, lon2, "-") / rho_lon
  dy <- outer(lat1, lat2, "-") / rho_lat
  
  r <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)
  
  K <- (1 + sqrt(3) * r) * exp(-sqrt(3) * r)
  
  return(K)
}

# Matérn-1/2 / Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Zeitkernel
# rho_time ist die zeitliche Längenskala in ka.
ou_time <- function(t1, t2, rho_time) {
  
  dt <- abs(outer(t1, t2, "-"))
  
  K <- exp(-dt / rho_time)
  
  return(K)
}

# Separabler Raum-Zeit-Kernel
st_kernel <- function(data1, data2, pars) {
  
  sigma_f <- pars$sigma_f
  rho_lon <- pars$rho_lon
  rho_lat <- pars$rho_lat
  rho_time <- pars$rho_time
  
  K_space <- matern32_space(
    lon1 = data1$lon,
    lat1 = data1$lat,
    lon2 = data2$lon,
    lat2 = data2$lat,
    rho_lon = rho_lon,
    rho_lat = rho_lat
  )
  
  K_time <- ou_time(
    t1 = data1$time_ka,
    t2 = data2$time_ka,
    rho_time = rho_time
  )
  
  K <- sigma_f^2 * K_space * K_time
  
  return(K)
}
```


```{r}
neg_loglik_gp <- function(theta, data) {
  
  # Parameter sind logarithmiert, damit sie positiv bleiben
  pars <- list(
    sigma_f  = exp(theta[1]),
    rho_lon  = exp(theta[2]),
    rho_lat  = exp(theta[3]),
    rho_time = exp(theta[4]),
    sigma_e  = exp(theta[5])
  )
  
  y <- data$resid
  n <- length(y)
  
  K <- st_kernel(data, data, pars)
  K <- K + diag(pars$sigma_e^2 + 1e-8, n)
  
  chol_K <- tryCatch(
    chol(K),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  if (is.null(chol_K)) {
    return(1e20)
  }
  
  alpha <- backsolve(chol_K, forwardsolve(t(chol_K), y))
  
  log_det <- 2 * sum(log(diag(chol_K)))
  
  nll <- 0.5 * sum(y * alpha) +
    0.5 * log_det +
    0.5 * n * log(2 * pi)
  
  return(nll)
}

```

```{r}
start_pars <- c(
  sigma_f  = sd(gp_train$resid, na.rm = TRUE),
  rho_lon  = 20,
  rho_lat  = 15,
  rho_time = 8,
  sigma_e  = 0.03
)

theta_start <- log(start_pars)

```

```{r}
############################################################
# 6. GP-Hyperparameter per Maximum Likelihood schätzen
#    mit Progress Bar
############################################################

library(progress)

# Achtung:
# optim() ruft die Zielfunktion oft häufiger auf als maxit,
# weil intern zusätzliche Funktionsauswertungen nötig sind.
# Daher ist total nur eine grobe Obergrenze.
max_eval <- 300

pb <- progress_bar$new(
  format = "GP-Fit [:bar] :percent | eval: :current/:total | nll: :nll | eta: :eta",
  total = max_eval,
  clear = FALSE,
  width = 80
)

eval_counter <- 0
last_nll <- NA_real_

neg_loglik_gp_progress <- function(theta, data) {
  
  nll <- neg_loglik_gp(theta, data)
  
  eval_counter <<- eval_counter + 1
  last_nll <<- nll
  
  if (eval_counter <= max_eval) {
    pb$tick(tokens = list(nll = round(nll, 2)))
  }
  
  return(nll)
}

fit_gp <- optim(
  par = theta_start,
  fn = neg_loglik_gp_progress,
  data = gp_train,
  method = "L-BFGS-B",
  control = list(
    maxit = 100
  )
)

# Falls optim mehr Funktionsauswertungen gebraucht hat als max_eval
cat("\nAnzahl Funktionsauswertungen:", eval_counter, "\n")

theta_hat <- fit_gp$par

pars_hat <- list(
  sigma_f  = exp(theta_hat[1]),
  rho_lon  = exp(theta_hat[2]),
  rho_lat  = exp(theta_hat[3]),
  rho_time = exp(theta_hat[4]),
  sigma_e  = exp(theta_hat[5])
)

cat("\nGeschätzte GP-Parameter:\n")
print(pars_hat)

cat("\nNegative Log-Likelihood:\n")
print(fit_gp$value)

theta_hat <- fit_gp$par

pars_hat <- list(
  sigma_f  = exp(theta_hat[1]),
  rho_lon  = exp(theta_hat[2]),
  rho_lat  = exp(theta_hat[3]),
  rho_time = exp(theta_hat[4]),
  sigma_e  = exp(theta_hat[5])
)

cat("\nGeschätzte GP-Parameter:\n")
print(pars_hat)

cat("\nNegative Log-Likelihood:\n")
print(fit_gp$value)

```

```{r}
predict_gp <- function(train_data, new_data, pars) {
  
  y <- train_data$resid
  n_train <- nrow(train_data)
  n_new <- nrow(new_data)
  
  K_train <- st_kernel(train_data, train_data, pars)
  K_train <- K_train + diag(pars$sigma_e^2 + 1e-8, n_train)
  
  K_cross <- st_kernel(train_data, new_data, pars)
  K_new <- st_kernel(new_data, new_data, pars)
  K_new <- K_new + diag(1e-8, n_new)
  
  L <- chol(K_train)
  
  alpha <- backsolve(L, forwardsolve(t(L), y))
  
  pred_mean <- as.vector(t(K_cross) %*% alpha)
  
  v <- forwardsolve(t(L), K_cross)
  
  pred_cov <- K_new - t(v) %*% v
  pred_var <- pmax(diag(pred_cov), 0)
  pred_sd <- sqrt(pred_var)
  
  out <- new_data %>%
    mutate(
      gp_mean = pred_mean,
      gp_sd = pred_sd,
      gp_lwr = gp_mean - 1.96 * gp_sd,
      gp_upr = gp_mean + 1.96 * gp_sd,
      pred_gam_gp = pred_gam + gp_mean,
      pred_gam_gp_lwr = pred_gam + gp_lwr,
      pred_gam_gp_upr = pred_gam + gp_upr
    )
  
  return(out)
}

```

```{r}
set.seed(456)

gp_test <- gp_df %>%
  sample_n(min(1000, nrow(gp_df)))

gp_pred <- predict_gp(
  train_data = gp_train,
  new_data = gp_test,
  pars = pars_hat
)

head(gp_pred)
```

```{r}
p_obs_pred <- ggplot(gp_pred, aes(x = tf, y = pred_gam_gp)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Beobachteter Tree Cover vs. GAM + GP-Vorhersage",
    x = "beobachteter Tree Cover",
    y = "Vorhersage: GAM + Residual-GP"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_obs_pred)
```

```{r}
gp_pred <- gp_pred %>%
  mutate(
    resid_gam = tf - pred_gam,
    resid_gam_gp = tf - pred_gam_gp
  )

p_resid_compare <- ggplot(gp_pred) +
  geom_density(aes(x = resid_gam), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_density(aes(x = resid_gam_gp), linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Residualverteilung: GAM vs. GAM + GP",
    subtitle = "Durchgezogene Linie: GAM-Residuen, gestrichelt: Residuen nach GP-Korrektur",
    x = "Residuum",
    y = "Dichte"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_resid_compare)
```


```{r}
############################################################
# Validierung: GAM vs. GAM + Residual-GP
############################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

############################################################
# 1. Falls noch keine eindeutige ID existiert
############################################################

gp_df <- gp_df %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number())

# Falls gp_train noch keine row_id hat, neu aus gp_df ziehen:
set.seed(123)

n_train <- 800

gp_train <- gp_df %>%
  sample_n(min(n_train, nrow(gp_df)))

############################################################
# 2. Unabhängiges Test-Subsample ziehen
############################################################

set.seed(456)

gp_test_pool <- gp_df %>%
  filter(!(row_id %in% gp_train$row_id))

n_test <- 1000

gp_test <- gp_test_pool %>%
  sample_n(min(n_test, nrow(gp_test_pool)))

cat("Trainingspunkte:", nrow(gp_train), "\n")
cat("Testpunkte:", nrow(gp_test), "\n")

############################################################
# 3. GP-Vorhersage auf Testdaten
############################################################

gp_pred <- predict_gp(
  train_data = gp_train,
  new_data = gp_test,
  pars = pars_hat
)

############################################################
# 4. Fehlermaße definieren
############################################################

rmse <- function(obs, pred) {
  sqrt(mean((obs - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
}

mae <- function(obs, pred) {
  mean(abs(obs - pred), na.rm = TRUE)
}

bias <- function(obs, pred) {
  mean(pred - obs, na.rm = TRUE)
}

############################################################
# 5. Vergleich GAM vs. GAM + GP
############################################################

validation_results <- tibble(
  model = c("GAM", "GAM + Residual-GP"),
  RMSE = c(
    rmse(gp_pred$tf, gp_pred$pred_gam),
    rmse(gp_pred$tf, gp_pred$pred_gam_gp)
  ),
  MAE = c(
    mae(gp_pred$tf, gp_pred$pred_gam),
    mae(gp_pred$tf, gp_pred$pred_gam_gp)
  ),
  Bias = c(
    bias(gp_pred$tf, gp_pred$pred_gam),
    bias(gp_pred$tf, gp_pred$pred_gam_gp)
  )
)

print(validation_results)

############################################################
# 6. Verbesserung in Prozent
############################################################

rmse_gam <- validation_results$RMSE[validation_results$model == "GAM"]
rmse_gp  <- validation_results$RMSE[validation_results$model == "GAM + Residual-GP"]

mae_gam <- validation_results$MAE[validation_results$model == "GAM"]
mae_gp  <- validation_results$MAE[validation_results$model == "GAM + Residual-GP"]

rmse_improvement <- 100 * (rmse_gam - rmse_gp) / rmse_gam
mae_improvement  <- 100 * (mae_gam - mae_gp) / mae_gam

cat("\nRMSE-Verbesserung durch GP:", round(rmse_improvement, 2), "%\n")
cat("MAE-Verbesserung durch GP :", round(mae_improvement, 2), "%\n")

############################################################
# 7. Residuen nach GAM und nach GAM + GP
############################################################

gp_pred <- gp_pred %>%
  mutate(
    resid_gam = tf - pred_gam,
    resid_gam_gp = tf - pred_gam_gp
  )

summary(gp_pred$resid_gam)
summary(gp_pred$resid_gam_gp)

############################################################
# 8. Plot: Beobachtet vs. Vorhergesagt
############################################################

p_obs_pred_gam <- ggplot(gp_pred, aes(x = tf, y = pred_gam)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Validierung: beobachteter Tree Cover vs. GAM",
    x = "beobachteter Tree Cover",
    y = "GAM-Vorhersage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_obs_pred_gam)

p_obs_pred_gp <- ggplot(gp_pred, aes(x = tf, y = pred_gam_gp)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Validierung: beobachteter Tree Cover vs. GAM + Residual-GP",
    x = "beobachteter Tree Cover",
    y = "GAM + GP-Vorhersage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_obs_pred_gp)

############################################################
# 9. Plot: Residuenvergleich
############################################################

p_resid_density <- ggplot(gp_pred) +
  geom_density(aes(x = resid_gam), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_density(aes(x = resid_gam_gp), linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Residualverteilung auf Testdaten",
    subtitle = "Durchgezogen: GAM-Residuen; gestrichelt: Residuen nach GP-Korrektur",
    x = "Residuum",
    y = "Dichte"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_resid_density)

############################################################
# 10. Plot: Residuen gegen Zeit
############################################################

p_resid_time <- ggplot(gp_pred, aes(x = time_ka)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = resid_gam), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_point(aes(y = resid_gam_gp), alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(
    title = "Residuen über die Zeit",
    subtitle = "Vergleich GAM vs. GAM + Residual-GP",
    x = "Zeit in ka BP",
    y = "Residuum"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_resid_time)

############################################################
# 11. Ergebnis speichern
############################################################

saveRDS(
  list(
    gp_train = gp_train,
    gp_test = gp_test,
    gp_pred = gp_pred,
    validation_results = validation_results,
    pars_hat = pars_hat
  ),
  file = "GP_validation_GAM_vs_GAM_GP.rds"
)

cat("\nValidierung gespeichert als: GP_validation_GAM_vs_GAM_GP.rds\n")
```
